_syncNote: v2026.626.0 cursor reconcile (PLA-1515). Upstream release v2026.626.0 (tag commit 4c6c0c6ad048838dda4a67e1aca43aa37a6fcf0d) was ALREADY merged into fork master out-of-band via PLA-1370 (merge commit 3a5f68d4c 'merge upstream/v2026.626.0 into sync/upstream-v2026.626.0'; branch head 1d024a1d, both are ancestors of origin/master). The weekly sync routine's state cursor was never advanced from v2026.618.0, so `node scripts/upstream-sync.mjs` no-oped every tick via the remoteBranchExists guard ('remote branch sync/upstream-v2026.626.0 exists but no open PR; leaving as-is for manual review'). This commit reconciles the durable cursor to reality so future ticks 304/no-op instead of re-flagging an already-landed sync. No content merge here — purely bookkeeping (mirrors 9f3a7799f/281dbdd37/c4f695194 post-merge cursor advances). Host release for v2026.626.0 already cut (fork.1..fork.7 line, PLA-1373 et al.), so the downstream host-release obligation is satisfied. lastSyncedSha pins the UPSTREAM tag commit per the established marker convention; the fork-master merge is 3a5f68d4c. etag captured from paperclipai/paperclip releases/latest at 2026-07-06 (strong validator). Supersedes v2026.618.0 (_syncNote from PLA-1270/PR #89).
